uncovered_requirements.text: layer-constants.cjs includes `b_to_f` as the 20th layer key
solve-wave-dag.cjs includes `b_to_f` in LAYER_DEPS with dependency on `t_to_c`
nf-solve.cjs diagnostic sweep computes b_to_f residual by classifying failing tests as covered_reproduced (0), covered_not_reproduced (+1), or not_covered (+1)
solve-remediate.md section 3a-extra dispatches close-formal-gaps for not_covered gaps (max 3/cycle) and model refinement for blind spots (max 2/cycle)
C→R scanner queries proximity-index.json for code file→requirement edges before flagging; score >= 0.6 auto-suppresses
`@requirement` annotations in source files (bin/*.cjs) are parsed by the proximity graph builder and create direct edges
/nf:debug Step A.5 runs formal model consultation before quorum dispatch, appending $FORMAL_CONTEXT to the worker bundle
Quorum worker prompts include a rule to respect fix constraints and not violate listed invariants
NEXT STEP result table includes a FORMAL row showing model verdict (reproduced/not-reproduced/no-model)
When no model covers the failure, debug Step G appends an entry to bug-model-gaps.json for future model creation
/nf:debug absorbs model-driven-fix Phases 1-4 (discovery, reproduction, refinement, constraint extraction)
Loop 1 (autoresearch-refine.cjs) runs natively within debug flow for bug reproduction
Extracted constraints from Loop 1 feed back into debug fix guidance and quorum worker prompts
Debug skill produces a reproducing formal model as a deliverable (not just a fix)
/nf:model-driven-fix deprecated — invocation prints deprecation notice routing to /nf:debug
solve-remediate b_to_f layer dispatches through /nf:debug (not deprecated /nf:model-driven-fix)
All consumers of model-driven-fix.md rewired to new integration points
Verification mode tagging — each model checker invocation is tagged as 'diagnostic' (Cycle 1, inverted semantics) or 'validation' (Cycle 2, normal semantics) to prevent mode contradiction
Trace comparison infrastructure — TLC counterexample traces parsed into structured JSON (ITF format) with state-by-state field extraction for diff generation
Configurable iteration limit — --max-iterations flag (default 3) controls both diagnostic refinement (Cycle 1) and simulation loop (Cycle 2), persisted in .planning/config.json
solve-classifications.json tracks per-scanner per-category FP rates across sessions (rolling window of last 10 sessions)
Scanners with FP rate > 60% over 5+ sessions auto-raise their suppression threshold by 0.1 (capped at 0.9)
`/nf:solve --report-only` displays per-scanner FP rate table in the diagnostics output
model-registry.json entries track `consecutive_pass_count` (incremented on pass, reset to 0 on fail) per solve session
Models with consecutive_pass_count >= 3 AND wiring >= 1.0 AND semantic >= 0.8 auto-promote from SOFT_GATE to HARD_GATE
Auto-promotion fires during solve Phase 4 (report) and logs to promotion-changelog.json with evidence snapshot
When a hypothesis transitions from UNMEASURABLE to CONFIRMED/VIOLATED, the layer it belongs to gets +1 priority weight in the next solve remediation wave ordering
solve-wave-dag.cjs reads hypothesis measurements and adjusts wave ordering to prioritize layers with recent transitions
When a quick task is triggered (`/nf:quick`), the workflow derives an APPROACH block (what will be done, what is explicitly out of scope) from the task description before spawning the planner
The APPROACH block is written to `.claude/scope-contract.json` with the task branch name as the key, making it available to the scope guard
The APPROACH block is non-modal — it is derived automatically from the task description text, not via a user dialog or confirmation step
close-formal-gaps.md accepts `--bug-context` flag that injects failure description into spec generation, biasing toward capturing the failure mode
Model refinement loop creates/refines a model, verifies it FAILS (reproduces the bug), and iterates up to 2 additional times if model passes despite bug
model-driven-fix.md workflow orchestrates the 6-phase prescriptive cycle (discovery, reproduction, refinement, constraint extraction, constrained fix, pre-verification)
`bin/extract-precedents.cjs` mines quorum debate archives for key decisions (BLOCK/APPROVE with reasoning) and writes `.planning/quorum/precedents.json`
Quorum dispatch prompts include up to 3 relevant precedents (matched by keyword overlap with current question)
Precedents have TTL (90 days default) and are auto-pruned
model-driven-fix.md Phase 5 runs model checkers on proximity-neighbor models (2-hop in proximity-index.json) before declaring fix done
run-formal-verify.cjs accepts `--scope=model1,model2` flag to run only specified models
bug-model-gaps.json entries include post_fix_verification results (model_pass, neighbor_models_pass, regressions array)
In `solve-diagnose`, after the existing Step 0e hypothesis synthesis, a quorum vote is dispatched on the root cause diagnosis before proceeding — requiring multi-model consensus on the causal chain
A new `nf-scope-guard.js` PreToolUse hook fires on Edit, Write, and MultiEdit tool calls and checks whether the target file is within the declared scope from `.claude/scope-contract.json`
When a file edit targets a path outside the declared scope, the scope guard emits a warning advisory via `additionalContext` (non-blocking, exits 0) — it does not deny the tool call
The scope guard is a no-op when no scope contract exists for the current session — the hook exits immediately without any output or overhead
Fix intent normalization — accept fix ideas as natural language, constraint sets, or code sketches and normalize internally to formal model mutations
Consequence model generation — given a reproducing model + normalized fix intent, generate a consequence model representing the system after the proposed fix is applied
Three-gate convergence — automated convergence check: (1) original invariants still hold, (2) bug no longer triggered (inverted check passes), (3) no 2-hop neighbor regressions
Simulation loop UX — Phase 4.5 in model-driven-fix.md with iteration display, per-gate pass/fail status, convergence progress, and escalation to user after max iterations
Test files containing `@requirement REQ-ID` annotations are excluded from T→R scanner flagging for those requirement IDs
`bin/annotate-tests.cjs` scans test files, uses proximity graph to suggest `@requirement` annotations, and writes them as comments
T→R scanner reports annotation coverage percentage alongside orphan test count
